title: Flavorful Snappy French Chicken Rillettes
description: A light, umami-packed French rillettes made from simmered chicken thighs in bone broth with bouillon cubes, finished with herbs and a splash of lemon for a snappy finish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large heavy‑bottom pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic cloves, chicken thighs, and thyme stems, browning the chicken on all sides for 3 minutes.
Pour in the bone broth with dissolved bouillon cubes, ensuring the chicken is mostly covered.
Raise the heat to a gentle boil, then reduce to a simmer and cover, cooking until the chicken is tender and easily shredded, about 40 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and let it sit covered for 10 minutes to deepen flavor.
Using two forks, shred the cooked chicken into a bowl, then stir in the lemon zest and season with salt and pepper.
Transfer the mixture into a clean jar, press down to eliminate air pockets, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.
